title: Uragoga violacea
uses: Uragoga violacea is known to have medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine. The plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is also believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of Uragoga violacea's medicinal applications.

side_effects: Uragoga violacea, also known as the "African snail plant" or "snail vine", has been reported to have various side effects when consumed. The plant contains toxic compounds that can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea in some individuals. Ingesting large quantities of Uragoga violacea can also lead to abdominal pain and headaches. Additionally, the plant may interact with certain medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Uragoga violacea for medicinal purposes.
